payload.answer: I found a shorthand/typo-heavy person-interaction note and can’t safely resolve it into a durable memory without confirmation.
citations: sam frm goldmn likes bill evns apparently
repair_options: Interpret as: "Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans" and keep it as a new memory, if that’s what you meant.
Interpret "frm goldmn" as a workplace/affiliation note and keep the original phrasing separately without resolving the abbreviation.
Interpret "bill evns" as the person Bill Evans, and keep that name as written.
If this is not a stable fact, keep it as an uncertain note rather than a committed memory.
If you meant different names or a different relation, provide the corrected wording.
